source: nginx access logs via promtail-prod -> staging Loki

request_samples.note: follow-up after redirect, served landing (6.8 kB)
TLS handshake sent to HTTP port, nginx bad-request
external probe UA='node', no trailing slash vs /api/decide route miss

interpretation.scanner_traffic: 3 of 6 hits (scan of /Dr0v, TLS noise, /api/decide probe burst)
interpretation.confirms_assumption: baseline RPS ~= 0 as expected for pre-launch prod; 30-min window sufficient to confirm

methodology_notes.no_prom_scrape_of_prod: by design (tunnel rejected); prod is observed via nginx access logs only. Internal state (cache hits, LND inflight, DB p95) is not visible at this step.
methodology_notes.request_time_distribution: nginx access_log format does not include $request_time on the default 'combined' format; promtail histogram metric is emitted but not scraped back (prod has no Prometheus exporter in scope). Latency at idle is not sampled in A2; A6 smoke test will provide iso-charge prod latency.
methodology_notes.prod_metrics_endpoint: /metrics is exposed and auth-bypassed from localhost (src/app.ts:408-416); unused in Phase 12A per scope decision.
methodology_notes.window_length_reduction: 30 min instead of 2 h; justified by near-zero baseline observed and confirmed on-the-fly.

downstream_use.A6_smoke_delta: subtract this baseline from A6 traffic to isolate smoke impact; given baseline is ~0, A6 numbers are effectively the smoke-only signal.
downstream_use.A7_report: feed the 'organic user load = 0' assumption into the report palier discussion (first user = rupture point at 1x is a real risk only under burst scenarios).
